title: Dry Your Meat
description: Always pat meat dry with paper towels before searing to get a perfect crust.

title: Don't Crowd the Pan
description: Too much food drops the temperature; food will boil instead of frying.

title: Season in Layers
description: Don't just salt at the end. Seasoning throughout builds a deeper flavor.

title: Rest Your Meat
description: Let cooked meat rest for 5-10 minutes to keep the juices inside.

title: Acid is the Key
description: If a dish tastes flat, add lemon or vinegar to brighten all other flavors.

title: Sharp Knives Only
description: Dull knives are more dangerous as they are more likely to slip.

title: Taste as You Go
description: The only way to know if it needs more seasoning is to taste it constantly.

title: Room Temp Eggs
description: Essential for perfect emulsions and fluffy results in baking.

title: Preheat Your Pan
description: Always heat your pan before adding oil and food to prevent sticking.

title: Save Pasta Water
description: The starchy 'liquid gold' helps your sauce stick perfectly to the noodles.

title: Freeze Your Scraps
description: Save onion skins and veggie tops in the freezer to make homemade broth.

title: Clean as You Go
description: Keeps the kitchen manageable and makes the cooking process enjoyable.

title: Use Fresh Spices
description: Spices lose flavor after 6 months. Replace them regularly for best taste.

title: Don't Peek in Oven
description: Opening the door drops the temperature significantly and ruins the rise.

title: Zest Before Juicing
description: It is much easier to zest a citrus fruit while it is still whole.

title: Use a Timer
description: Never rely on memory. Use a timer to avoid overcooking your precious meals.

title: Keep a Trash Bowl
description: Place a bowl on your counter for scraps to save trips to the bin.

title: Wet Towel Trick
description: Put a damp paper towel under your board to keep it from sliding.

title: Rotate Your Trays
description: Most ovens have hot spots. Rotate your trays halfway through baking.

title: Measure Twice
description: Especially in baking, accuracy is everything. Double check your measurements.
